accurate.why: All claims align with the bill (CRA disapproval voiding the DOE rule) and accurate context about the rule verified via the Federal Register and GAO records.

no_hallucinations.why: Contextual details (DOE administration, rule content, deferred compliance dates for non-display doors and refrigeration systems) are accurate real-world facts, not fabrications.

states_purpose.why: Clearly states the resolution nullifies the DOE walk-in coolers/freezers energy conservation standards rule.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Identifies that it nullifies the specific DOE rule (89 FR 104616) and describes the standards being voided.

exceptions_conditions.why: A one-line CRA disapproval has no material exceptions or carve-outs to note.

effective_dates.why: Notes the rule's submission date (Dec 23, 2024) and the rule's compliance deadlines, consistent with the rule's deferred dates.

major_provisions_covered.why: Captures the essential operative effect: the rule is disapproved and has no force or effect.

objective_tone.why: Neutral and descriptive with no advocacy or editorializing.

concise.why: Compact with no padding or boilerplate.

correct_entities.why: Correctly names the Department of Energy and the precise rule title.

correct_figures.why: The submission date (Dec 23, 2024) is correct and the stated compliance deadlines for non-display doors and refrigeration systems match the rule's deferred dates.
